channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Lauren Sanchez alludes to ‘turbulence’ in life after ‘obscene’ Trump inauguration display
item.description: The fiancee of billionaire Jeff Bezos alluded to a rough start of 2025, several weeks after Megyn Kelly called her a 'hooker' for wearing a lacy white bustier top beneath a jacket to Trump's swearing-in.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: Streaming is finally profitable. It offers a lesson in patience
item.description: Just a couple of years ago, pundits were warning of streaming’s demise. From Netflix to Spotify, these companies were burning through cash. How could they keep operating? Now, almost all of the streamers have made it to positive profits.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Audacious Bid to Buy OpenAI's Nonprofit Arm Sparks Concern Among Tech Leaders
item.description: Elon Musk-led group launches $97.4 billion bid for OpenAI's nonprofit arm, sparking concerns among tech leaders and highlighting the complexities of modern-day business and politics.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Ukraine's rare earth elements could help keep US military aid flowing
item.description: Ukraine has offered to strike a deal with U.S. President Donald Trump for continued American military aid in exchange for developing Ukraine’s mineral industry.
item.dc:creator: Samya Kullab, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: AI summit in Paris brings together Big Tech and U.S. VP Vance
item.description: Major world leaders are meeting for an AI summit in Paris, where challenging diplomatic talks are expected as tech titans fight for dominance in the fast-moving technology industry.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: News influencers favored Trump over Harris during campaign, study finds
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Americans who followed news influencers during the presidential campaign were more likely to hear positive reports about Donald Trump than they were about Kamala Harris, a study has revealed.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Meta layoffs to begin as company moves to cut 5 percent of workforce
item.description: The layoffs, according to Zuckerberg, would target "low-performers." The layoffs will amount to roughly 3,600 people losing their jobs, according to a report.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, NewsNation

item.title: How AI could strengthen citizens’ confidence in government
item.description: "There are some very prominent ones that we see every day, things like at the TSA with facial recognition," said Valerie Wirtschafter. The post How AI could strengthen citizens’ confidence in government first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Musk furious as judge shuts down DOGE access to Treasury payment system
item.description: The US Treasury Department’s payment servers hold the tax returns, social security data and bank account numbers of every adult citizen of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Internet upgrade part of a move towards 400/800G connectivity
item.description: The industry transition to 400 Gigabit Ethernet networking took a big step forward this week when the world’s leading Internet exchange operator announced plans to upgrade its New York backbone to 400G.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Ex-Treasury secretaries express worries over DOGE payment systems
item.description: Former Treasury Secretaries penned an op-ed Monday expressing their concerns that officials at Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) gained access to the federal payment systems.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Is your camera ticket a fake? Here’s how to tell
item.description: Red light camera tickets: Not liable to ID the driver; some are fishing expeditions. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson helps you fight back against tricky fake tickets.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: When LLMs become influencers
item.description: On Sunday people tuned in to watch the Super Bowl—and lots and lots of ads. Online, however, it’s increasingly the case that AI agents will see and interact with ads. Humans won’t.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Notre Dame scores big with Wi-Fi 6E stadium upgrade
item.description: When 80,000 fans fill the University of Notre Dame’s iconic football stadium on Saturday afternoons in the fall to cheer on the Fighting Irish, there’s no shortage of high-decibel screaming. And now, thanks to a major network expansion, there’s no shortage of high-bandwidth streaming.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump's AI ambition and China's DeepSeek overshadow an AI summit in Paris
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The geopolitics of artificial intelligence will be in focus at a major summit in France where world leaders, executives and experts will hammer out pledges on guiding the development of the rapidly advancing technology.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: Java Development Kit (JDK) 24 has entered the release candidate stage. The next version of standard Java will ship with 24 features, ranging from a class-file API to a fourth preview of structured concurrency. The first JDK 24 release candidate arrived February 6, following two rampdown stages.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Lawmakers turn to IGs to investigate DOGE access to agency data
item.description: Democrats are turning to inspector general's offices, including those swept up in Trump's firing of several IG's last month, to investigate DOGE's data access. The post Lawmakers turn to IGs to investigate DOGE access to agency data first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: How the slash-and-burn tactics Musk brought to Washington backfired at Twitter
item.description: When Elon Musk bought Twitter in 2022, he laid off thousands of employees, stopped paying rent and auctioned off coffee makers and office chairs in hopes of a big turnaround.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Amazon to pay nearly $4M to settle lawsuit alleging it took tips from drivers
item.description: Amazon has agreed to pay nearly $4 million to settle charges that the e-commerce company subsidized its labor costs by taking tips its delivery drivers received from customers, District of Columbia Attorney General Brian L. Schwalb said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Robot dog sprints into history books by breaking speed records
item.description: A Chinese startup has developed a robot, Mirror Me, that has run about 100 meters in under 10 seconds, pushing the boundaries of robotics and speed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How AI could replace government workers to reduce spending
item.description: Elon Musk and DOGE are reportedly using artificial intelligence to analyze federal spending and where cuts an be made
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: LG recalls 500K electric ovens after fires, pet deaths
item.description: According to the recall, the front-mounted knobs on the recalled ranges can be accidentally activated by humans or pets.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, NewsNation

item.title: Full-stack JavaScript leads the way
item.description: Building web apps begins with a simple premise, but the real-world experience is a juggling act of optimizations, best practices, toolchains, and so on. Developers tend to respond by doing what we do best: creating new tools and techniques to manage ever-changing demands.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenAI looks across US for sites to build its Trump-backed Stargate AI data centers
item.description: OpenAI is scouring the U.S. for sites to build a network of huge data centers to power its artificial intelligence technology, expanding beyond a flagship Texas location and looking across 16 states to accelerate the Stargate project championed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Nebraska government system shutdown disrupts the Legislature, DMV and other offices
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — A systemwide outage of state goverment computers shut down everything from the work of the Legislature to driver’s licensing offices for several hours Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MARGERY A. BECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lawmakers warn hiring freeze could thwart cyber workforce efforts
item.description: A former White House official who led cyber and tech recruiting efforts said the hiring freeze "will set us back months if not years." The post Lawmakers warn hiring freeze could thwart cyber workforce efforts first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to block your phone from tracking your location
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Smartphones are useful tools for everyday life, but they're privy to nearly everything about you, including all the places you've been — if you let them.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
